item.title: Scaling BioLNG and eLNG for Maritime Sector Decarbonisation in Europe - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: Why Emissions Allocation Must Follow the Biomethane/e-Methane Chain of Custody in the Recast Implementing Regulation on Sustainability Certification

item.title: Europe’s growing biomethane sector highlights the strategic role of gas infrastructure - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: The latest European Biomethane Map confirms that renewable gas production is expanding rapidly, but unlocking its full value will depend on Europe’s infrastructure and market integration

item.title: Industry organisations call for Adequate Treatment of Hydrogen in the EU Grids Package - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: As Europe seeks to strengthen competitiveness, energy security and decarbonisation, hydrogen infrastructure will be essential, alongside electricity networks, in building an integrated and resilient energy system.

item.title: Beyond filling targets: GIE calls for a European toolbox to unlock the strategic value of gas storage - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: As the European Commission prepares its upcoming Energy Security Package, the debate is increasingly shifting beyond short-term filling targets towards the longer-term role of gas storage in safeguarding Europe against systemic shocks and supply disruptions.

item.title: New study: Unlocking energy system efficiency with hydrogen - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: On 14 April 2026 , Gas Infrastructure Europe (GIE) hosted a workshop in Brussels to present a new study highlighting the importance of coordinated planning for electricity, hydrogen and gas infrastructure for a secure, affordable and climate‑neutral European energy system. The study sets out key insights relevant to the future evolution of Europe’s energy policy and Security of Supply framework.

item.title: Europe’s winter gas readiness back in focus - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: Europe’s gas security of supply is once again under scrutiny as storage levels remain low and market conditions limit early injections. With the coming winter in view, ensuring timely storage filling, sufficient LNG supplies and full use of Europe’s gas infrastructure will be key to maintaining system resilience.

item.title: New GSE EU Storage outlook study - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: Commissioned by GIE, the study combines a pan‑European, multi‑energy modelling assessment by Artelys with an in‑depth regulatory analysis by Compass Lexecon. It provides a quantitative basis to inform the upcoming revision of European security‑of‑supply rules, reflecting the growing integration of gas, electricity, and hydrogen systems.

item.title: Joint position taxation - EC recommendations - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: GIE joins a broad coalition of energy and industrial stakeholders in a joint position on the European Commission’s draft Energy Taxation recommendations. The statement urges reforms to ensure fairer, more predictable energy bills and avoid double taxation, supporting affordability, competitiveness, and a just transition.

item.title: New Joint Statement on EU Permitting Reform Published - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: The Informal Coalition on Permitting has released a new joint statement calling for structural improvements to Europe’s permitting framework. The document outlines cross‑industry priorities to accelerate project deployment and strengthen Europe’s competitiveness.

item.title: Luis Ignacio Parada reappointed as GLE President - Gas Infrastructure Europe
item.description: Gas Infrastructure Europe (GIE) is pleased to announce the re-election of Luis Ignacio Parada as President of Gas LNG Europe (GLE) for a second two-year term, ensuring continuity in the association’s strategic direction for Europe’s LNG infrastructure.

item.title: From Vacuum to Lab: A Student’s Journey Towards Real-World Application – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: In the classroom, students have the opportunity to learn and practice specific concepts in a controlled situation, sometimes referred to as a vacuum. Yet in the real-world, many concepts and skills must be considered simultaneously to arrive at a solution. Whitney Walpole, a mechanical engineering student at ASPIRE, is discovering what it takes to answer real-world questions.

item.title: From Student to Alumni: Software Engineer Makes a Real-World Impact at ASPIRE – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: Managing a fleet of electric buses requires tracking not only the buses but also the electric vehicle chargers themselves in order to track overall purchasing and maintenance costs and power loads. With so many moving parts, this can be difficult and time consuming to do. However, Max Ramsdell, a software engineer at ASPIRE, led a team to find a way to make it easier for electric fleets.

item.title: Partner Spotlight: Measuring the Impact of ASPIRE’s EWD Efforts – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: Understanding the long-term impact of electrification workforce development programs is essential to preparing the next generation of engineers and transportation leaders. Working with ASPIRE, the Research, Evaluation, and Assessment Services (REAS) Center at The University of Texas at El Paso (UTEP) has partnered with the project to evaluate K–12 education preparedness, STEM outreach, and career pathway outcomes.

item.title: Best Paper Award: A Collaborative Effort to a Researcher’s Milestone – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: When up against a couple hundred other research papers, it can be difficult for your research to stand out. Yet standout is exactly what one Utah State University research team from ASPIRE did last month at an electrified transportation conference with their paper, “Modeling and Stability Analysis of a Modular IPOS-Connected DC Current Distribution System for EV In-Motion Wireless Charging System.”

item.title: July ’26 ASPIRE Student Section – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: Check out the new Council of Presidents, student highlights, cohort trip, and available travel awards below!

item.title: Reaching the ‘A-ha’ Moment: Inspiring STEM Life-Long Learners Through Future Teachers – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: Innovative technologies are paving the way to a technologically advanced future, and a skilled and motivated workforce is essential to fueling this progress forward. Using new K-12 resources posted on ASPIRE’s Teach Engineering (TE), two professors from The University of Texas at El Paso (UTEP) are doing just that.

item.title: Prepping the Future Workforce: ASPIRE’s High School Internships – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: What should I study? What career do I want to pursue?

item.title: Unlocking the Bigger Picture: ASPIRE Student Cohort Trip Participants Discover the Broader Fields of Electrification – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: Student researchers at ASPIRE are diving deep to understand complex technology and find efficient solutions to pave the way to an electrified future. These efforts are part of a global push to leave the earth better for future generations, though it can be easy to lose sight of the global impact and systems that relate to highly specified technical research. But by visiting industry members in Washington, D.C., and Utah as part of the annual student cohort trip, 12 students from across ASPIRE’s campuses got to explore the possibilities in a new light.

item.title: June ’26 Market Pulse – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: Market Overview: The global electric vehicle market entered Q2 2026 navigating a complex landscape of geopolitical shifts, pricing pressures, and structural changes. A major driver this quarter has been the regional conflict in the Middle East, which pushed oil prices past $100 a barrel and noticeably altered consumer sentiment. While higher fuel costs have historically driven buyers toward electrification to lower long-term transit expenses, the domestic and international markets are reacting in sharply different ways. Globally, long-term momentum remains substantial, with the IEA’s latest Global EV Outlook projecting that electric and plug-in hybrid cars will account for nearly 30% of all vehicles sold worldwide this year.

item.title: Collaboration Across Disciplines: The Grid of Tomorrow Consortium – ASPIRE Research Center
item.description: With the recent breakthroughs in technology for electrified vehicles and roadways, coupled with growth in AI and data centers, the transportation-grid edge is rapidly changing. This evolution doesn’t merely affect a single industry; rather, its impact spans across disciplines, requiring increased collaboration. Researchers, including several from ASPIRE, have founded the Grid of Tomorrow Consortium (GoTC), headquartered at Purdue University, to provide this vital opportunity for multi-disciplinary problem-solving.
